Your Personal Morning Dashboard

James' Morning Briefing

Thursday, April 2, 2026
Top Story

Trump Threatens to Destroy Iran's Desalination Plants in Escalating War

President Trump has renewed threats to 'blow up' Iran's civilian infrastructure, including desalination plants that provide drinking water to millions. In a prime-time address, Trump claimed military success but offered no clear timeline to end fighting. Iran has been targeting Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones, and tensions remain high as the US-Israel war on Iran continues.

Read Full Story →

World News

Trump Threatens to Destroy Iran's Desalination Plants in Escalating War

President Trump has renewed threats to 'blow up' Iran's civilian infrastructure, including desalination plants that provide drinking water to millions. In a prime-time address, Trump claimed military success but offered no clear timeline to end fighting. Iran has been targeting Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones, and tensions remain high as the US-Israel war on Iran continues.

Al Jazeera / NPR / AP News · News · Read more

NASA's Artemis II Successfully Launches - First Crewed Moon Mission Since 1972

NASA's Artemis II rocket blasted off from Kennedy Space Center on April 1, 2026, carrying four astronauts on a 10-day mission around the Moon. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen. This marks humanity's first crewed flight beyond low Earth orbit since Apollo 17 in 1972.

NASA / The Guardian / CNN · News · Read more

Russia-Ukraine War: Russia Rejects Zelenskyy's Easter Truce Offer with Drone Attacks

Russia responded to Ukrainian President Zelenskyy's Easter truce offer with a massive drone attack, launching 700 drones in two waves. The war has reached day 1,499 with Russian forces having captured approximately 1,927 square miles of Ukrainian territory over the past year. A Russian military transport plane also crashed in Crimea, killing 29.

The Guardian / Institute for the Study of War · News · Read more

Iran Targets Gulf Countries with Ballistic Missiles and Drones

Iran continued targeting Gulf states including Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE with ballistic missiles and drones. Thousands gathered in Tehran for the funeral of Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the IRGC navy, killed in US-Israeli strikes. Death toll estimates include 1,937 dead in Iran, at least 24 in Israel, 13 US soldiers and 27 killed in Gulf states.

Al Jazeera / NPR / Reuters · News · Read more

Seattle News

undefined° / undefined°
undefined
[object Object]

Public Hearing for 2026 Library Levy Today

The Seattle City Council is holding a public hearing on Thursday, April 2 for the 2026 Library Levy. The levy proposes a $410 million investment in critical funding for Seattle Public Libraries. Members of the public can participate in the hearing to provide input on the proposed funding package.

undefined · Read more

Mariners Face Yankees in Series Finale

The Seattle Mariners are playing the New York Yankees on Wednesday, April 1 at 4:10 PM ET at T-Mobile Park, attempting to break a 1-1 series tie. The Mariners opened their 2026 season on March 26 against the Cleveland Guardians. Notable prospects Kade Anderson and Ryan Sloan will both begin the 2026 season in Double-A Arkansas.

undefined · Read more

WSDOT Revive I-5 Construction Continues

WSDOT's Revive I-5 project continues in 2026 with long-term lane reductions and closures. Express lanes are currently running northbound only, causing potential delays for southbound commuters. A full weekend closure of northbound I-5 from I-90 to NE 45th St is planned for late 2026. SDOT has implemented a 5-part strategy to help keep traffic moving during construction.

undefined · Read more

2026 Washington Legislative Session Recap

The 60-day 2026 Washington legislative session adjourned on March 12 at 8:26 PM after a grueling final week that included a 24.5-hour continuous floor debate on March 9-10 over a proposed state income tax. Key outcomes include expanded property tax relief for seniors, disabled homeowners, and veterans starting with 2027 property tax statements. The last day for the governor to take action on bills is April 4, 2026.

undefined · Read more

Technology

OpenAI Raises $122 Billion at $852 Billion Valuation

OpenAI has completed the largest funding round in tech history, raising $122 billion at an $852 billion valuation. The funding will power the company's push for more chips, data centers, and talent. OpenAI is reportedly generating $2 billion in monthly revenue and planning a potential IPO.

The Mercury News / NYT · News · Read more

Q1 2026 Shatters Venture Funding Records: AI Startups Raise $297 Billion

The first quarter of 2026 saw unprecedented venture investment, with AI companies raising $297 billion globally—2.5x more than the previous quarter. AI now accounts for 50% of all private funding worldwide, with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Waymo leading the charge.

Crunchbase News · News · Read more

NVIDIA Invests $2 Billion in Marvell for AI Chip Partnership

NVIDIA announced a major strategic partnership with Marvell Technology, investing $2 billion to co-develop custom XPUs, NVLink Fusion interconnects, and silicon photonics. The deal extends NVIDIA's portfolio beyond GPUs as it moves to own the full AI stack.

Reuters · News · Read more

NVIDIA's China Market Share Falls Below 60% as Domestic Chips Rise

NVIDIA's AI chip market share in China has dropped to 55%, down from 95% before sanctions. Chinese chip makers delivered 1.65 million AI GPUs in Q1 2026 as the government pushes data centers to use domestic alternatives. NVIDIA shipped an estimated 2.2 million cards but faces increasing competition.

Tom's Hardware / StartupNews · News · Read more
